clinical_history: This ECG from an elderly woman shows a short pause. What is its mechanism?
diagnosis: The ECG shows predominant sinus rhythm with a single blocked atrial premature complex (APC). If you look carefully, you will see the T wave of the second beat is slightly deformed by a superimposed P´ wave. The total QRS-QRS pause is about 1.4 sec. (The sinus P waves, themselves, are at the upper limits of normal in size/duration, raising question of left atrial abnormality. By echocardiogram, the left atrium was also at the upper normal limits in size.) The precordial leads show borderline low QRS voltage, a non-specific finding.
